q: How does tissue fluid form at the arterial end of a capillary?
options: Active transport pushes plasma out of the capillary into surrounding tissues
Osmotic pressure exceeds hydrostatic pressure, drawing fluid into the capillary
Hydrostatic pressure of blood exceeds osmotic pressure, forcing plasma out through fenestrations
White blood cells rupture and release their contents into the interstitial space
explanation: At the arterial end, blood pressure (hydrostatic) is higher than the osmotic pressure pulling fluid back in, so net movement is outward into the interstitial space.
